title: Attention Is All You Need
description: The foundational paper introducing the Transformer architecture, which has become the backbone of modern NLP and generative AI systems including GPT and BERT.

title: Hugging Face Transformers Library
description: State-of-the-art machine learning library providing thousands of pretrained models for NLP, computer vision, audio, and multimodal tasks.

title: fast.ai Practical Deep Learning for Coders
description: A free course designed for people with some coding experience who want to learn how to apply deep learning and machine learning to practical problems.

title: LangChain Framework
description: A framework for developing applications powered by large language models, including chains, agents, and retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.

title: The Illustrated Transformer
description: An intuitive visual guide to understanding the Transformer model architecture, breaking down self-attention and multi-head attention with clear diagrams.

title: OpenAI API
description: Access to GPT-4, DALL-E, Whisper, and other OpenAI models through a robust API platform for building AI-powered applications.

title: 3Blue1Brown Neural Networks Series
description: A beautifully animated video series explaining neural networks from the ground up, covering gradient descent, backpropagation, and deep learning fundamentals.

title: Papers With Code
description: A platform that links machine learning papers with their official code implementations, datasets, and benchmark results across thousands of tasks.

title: The Batch Newsletter by Andrew Ng
description: A weekly newsletter covering the most important AI news, research breakthroughs, and practical insights curated by Andrew Ng and the DeepLearning.AI team.

title: Common Crawl Dataset
description: A massive open repository of web crawl data, widely used for training large language models. Contains petabytes of raw web page data, metadata, and text extractions.

title: Practical AI Podcast
description: A podcast focused on making artificial intelligence practical, productive, and accessible to everyone, featuring interviews with leading AI practitioners.

title: Stanford CS229: Machine Learning
description: Complete lecture notes and materials from Stanford's flagship machine learning course covering supervised learning, unsupervised learning, and learning theory.

title: Andrej Karpathy: Let's Build GPT
description: A detailed 2-hour walkthrough of building a GPT language model from scratch in Python, explaining every component of the transformer architecture along the way.

title: r/MachineLearning
description: The largest machine learning community on Reddit with over 3 million members discussing papers, projects, career advice, and the latest developments in AI.

title: Coursera Machine Learning Specialization
description: A comprehensive 3-course specialization taught by Andrew Ng, covering supervised learning, advanced algorithms, and unsupervised learning with hands-on labs.

title: Self Hosting AIs for Research
description: A comprehensive guide from the University of South Florida Library on how to host AI models locally for privacy and research purposes.

title: Claude Prompting Best Practices
description: Official documentation providing best practices for engineering effective prompts specifically for Anthropic's Claude models.

title: Gemini API Prompt Gallery Tutorial
description: A collection of prompt examples and tutorials for getting started with the Gemini API across various use cases.

title: Consensus AI
description: An AI search engine that finds answers to research questions based on peer-reviewed scientific papers.

title: Scite.ai
description: A citation discovery platform that uses AI to provide context on how research papers have been cited by others (supporting, contrasting, or mentioning).

title: Elicit
description: An AI research assistant that automates parts of the literature review process, such as summarizing papers and extracting specific data points.

title: STORM (Wikipedia-like report with AI)
description: A system that can generate a comprehensive Wikipedia-like report on any given topic by interacting with various sources to synthesize information.

title: NotebookLM (Google)
description: A personalized AI notebook that helps you synthesize information from your own documents and notes using Gemini models.

title: Google Scholar Labs
description: Experimental tools from Google Scholar designed to improve how researchers find, organize, and explore academic literature.

title: Research Rabbit
description: A visual discovery tool for academic papers that helps researchers map out connections between publications, citations, and authors.

title: AI for Science - Nature News
description: A news analysis exploring the role of AI models in accelerating scientific discovery and data interpretation.

title: Prompt Engineering Guide
description: A comprehensive, community-driven guide for learning prompt engineering techniques across various large language models.

title: Dataskeptic Podcast
description: A podcast exploring the world of data science, statistics, and artificial intelligence with experts in the field.

title: AI ‘Scientists’ Accelerating Research
description: A discussion on how autonomous AI 'scientists' are being designed and used to accelerate the pace of research discoveries.

title: RAG Assistant for Zotero
description: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) assistant for Zotero that allows users to interact with their library of research papers using LLMs.

title: AI Fluency: Framework & Foundations
description: A learning path designed by Anthropic to build a foundation of AI fluency, covering concepts and frameworks for effective interaction with AI systems.

title: Awesome AI Tutorials
description: A curated collection of high-quality tutorials covering various aspects of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and deep learning.

title: AI for Research and Insights
description: A Google-led course on Coursera focused on using AI tools to gain deeper research insights and streamline the academic workflow.

title: Ethics of AI (MIT OCW)
description: A Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) OpenCourseWare series exploring the ethical implications, biases, and sociotechnical challenges of AI.

title: Elements of AI
description: An introductory course on the fundamentals of artificial intelligence designed for non-experts to understand its impact and basics.

title: Generative AI for Everyone
description: A non-technical introductory course by Andrew Ng and DeepLearning.AI on the capabilities, limitations, and applications of generative AI.

title: AI For Everyone (Coursera)
description: A popular non-technical course by Andrew Ng providing a high-level overview of AI, machine learning, and how it's used in the real world.

title: AI in Academic Research: Introduction
description: A comprehensive guide from Stanford University Library on how to use AI tools effectively for academic research and information discovery.

title: Prompt Engineering Techniques
description: A repository containing a comprehensive collection of prompt engineering techniques, templates, and best practices for various LLMs.

title: LM Studio Tutorial
description: A detailed DataCamp guide on how to use LM Studio, an application that allows you to run LLMs locally on your own hardware.

title: HAI Stanford Blog
description: The news feed for the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered AI (HAI), featuring updates on research, ethics, and policy in AI.

title: Effortless Academic
description: A repository of resources and tools designed to help students and academics simplify their workflow using modern technology and automation.

title: ExplainPaper
description: An AI-powered platform that helps researchers break down and understand complex academic papers through summaries and interactive explanations.

title: ResearchCollab
description: A specialized platform that utilizes AI to provide deep insights into scientific papers and foster collaboration among researchers.

title: Moara AI
description: An advanced AI search engine and discovery tool tailored for academic researchers to navigate vast bodies of literature efficiently.

title: SciSpace
description: Provides interactive PDF reading assistance and multi-source context retrieval through an AI-powered research co-pilot.

title: Prompt Engineering: Overview and Guide
description: An official educational resource from Google Cloud explaining the core concepts, techniques, and additional learning paths for mastering prompt engineering.

title: Litmaps
description: A visual timeline mapping tool for citation networks, helping researchers see how research evolves over time.

title: Connected Papers
description: A visual tool that discovers and maps research fields and clusters from a single starting seed paper.

title: Open Knowledge Maps
description: Maps keyword queries to visual clusters of semantic relevance, helping to visualize the structure of a research topic.

title: Paperpal
description: A comprehensive grammar check and academic tone editing tool specifically designed for research manuscripts.

title: R Discovery
description: Curates daily personalized academic research feeds and simplifies the literature search process for active researchers.

title: Ollama
description: A CLI-first local model server that allows users to run LLMs locally and act as an API backend.

title: LM Studio (App)
description: A highly polished local desktop application for searching and running a wide variety of local LLMs with a user-friendly interface.

title: Jan
description: A fully open-source and file-over-app local LLM desktop application designed for easy installation and use.

title: GPT4All
description: A private, offline AI application designed specifically for use on consumer laptops with CPU-only hardware.

title: NextChat
description: A custom-masked web and desktop interface for LLMs that saves conversational logs locally in the browser.

title: AnythingLLM
description: An all-in-one RAG workspace that supports no-code agent construction and team administration for collaborative AI work.

title: GPTZero
description: An advanced AI scan tool offering writing replays and citation verification to detect machine-generated content.

title: The Sentient Syllabus Project
description: A collaborative network providing open-access course syllabus templates and language for AI-integrated education.

title: Derek Bruff's Intentional Teaching Resource
description: Actionable strategies for designing class expectations around the integration of AI tools in educational settings.

title: Ryan Watkins' Medium Guide
description: A step-by-step primer on adapting higher education syllabus policies to account for and incorporate LLMs.

title: One Useful Thing
description: Newsletter by Wharton Professor Ethan Mollick featuring hands-on model tests and strategic essays on AI application.

title: Critical AI
description: A scholarly interdisciplinary journal investigating artificial intelligence from diverse humanities perspectives.

title: The Batch
description: A weekly analytical digest covering business, education, and global policy impacts of AI.

title: Import AI
description: Newsletter by Jack Clark tracking frontier research, AI governance, and technical developments in the field.

title: AI Snake Oil
description: A newsletter exposing benchmark anomalies and the predictive failures of current commercial AI systems.

title: Marcus on AI
description: Commentary from Gary Marcus focusing on the cognitive limitations of scaling modern AI models.

title: Grading for Growth
description: Weekly articles advocating for process-centric and alternative evaluation workflows in education.

title: Ethics in AI (Oxford)
description: Oxford University's multidisciplinary seminars on human autonomy and technology ethics.

title: Ethical AI Podcast (IU)
description: Indiana University faculty conversations addressing bias, equity, and institutional data policies in AI.

title: On Tech Ethics
description: CITI Program podcast focusing on academic integrity and research ethics in the age of LLMs.

title: Generative AI for Educators & Teachers
description: Vanderbilt University's training course exploring classroom prompt engineering and safety for educators.

title: Generative AI for Educators Specialization
description: IBM Skills Network course exploring advanced prompting patterns and performance assessments for educators.

title: DEC Certificate in AI for Higher Education
description: Fully scalable certificate delivering actionable institutional governance frameworks for AI in higher education.

title: Generative AI and ChatGPT for K-12 Educators
description: Vanderbilt University's program focusing on building classroom problem-solving strategies using GenAI.

title: AI for Educators Learning Path
description: Microsoft Learn's self-paced sequence on integrating educational Copilot tools into teaching workflows.

title: Teaching and Learning with Generative AI
description: DePaul University's program on syllabus alignment, academic integrity, and course redesign for the AI era.
